questions.question: The diagram shows a root hair cell from a lemon tree and some soil particles.
questions.text: The diagram shows a root hair cell from a lemon tree and some soil particles.

parts.text: State one feature, visible in the diagram, which shows that a root hair cell is a plant cell.
Describe how mineral ions pass from the soil into the root hair cell.
The lemon tree has grown in the same soil for many years so the soil now lacks magnesium. Describe and explain the effect on the lemon tree of a lack of magnesium.

questions.question: A person visits an eye doctor to have an eye test. On the wall of the doctor's room is a diagram of an eye.
questions.text: A person visits an eye doctor to have an eye test. On the wall of the doctor's room is a diagram of an eye.

parts.text: Name the part labelled X.
Describe one way in which the structure of X is related to its function.
Name layer Y.
Name structure Z.
Layer Y does not contain blood vessels. Explain how the blood vessels in the choroid layer are important for layer Y.

When the bright light is shone into the eye there is a pupil reflex which causes the pupil to become smaller. Explain how named parts of the eye and of the nervous system are involved in this pupil reflex.

Some people inherit a condition which prevents the production of the dark pigment melanin in their choroid and skin cells. The gene involved has two alleles, A (dominant) and a (recessive). Only people with two recessive alleles have this condition. Two people, each with a heterozygous genotype, have children together. Complete the genetic diagram to show the possible genotypes and phenotypes of the children.
State the probability of these two people having a child who does not produce melanin.

questions.question: A sycamore tree is a producer for many food chains in a forest ecosystem.
questions.text: A sycamore tree is a producer for many food chains in a forest ecosystem.

parts.text: Explain what is meant by a producer.
Explain what is meant by a food chain.
The diagrams show a pyramid of numbers and a pyramid of biomass for a sycamore tree. Explain the different shapes of the two pyramids.
Sycamore trees produce flowers that are insect-pollinated. Suggest two features of flowers which adapt them for insect-pollination.
State the type of cell division that produces pollen grains.

questions.question: The diagram shows part of the human alimentary canal and its associated organs.
questions.text: The diagram shows part of the human alimentary canal and its associated organs.

parts.text: Complete the three labels on the diagram by naming the structures.
Describe the functions of the structure labelled F.
Name structure H and describe its role in homeostasis.

questions.question: A farmer is using a tool, called a mattock, to weed her crop.
questions.text: A farmer is using a tool, called a mattock, to weed her crop.

parts.text: Explain how the muscles and bones in her right arm help her to lift the tool.
The farmer has not used any insecticides or artificial fertilisers on the crop. Explain ways in which this may help the local environment.
Outline the role of microorganisms in the process of yoghurt production.
Yoghurt can contribute to a balanced diet but adult humans cannot survive by eating only yoghurt. Explain this statement using the information on the label.

questions.question: Excess amino acids are broken down in liver cells to form molecules of urea.
questions.text: Excess amino acids are broken down in liver cells to form molecules of urea.

parts.text: Describe the pathway taken by a molecule of urea, from the liver of a mammal until it reaches the soil.
Urea is a molecule which contains nitrogen. Suggest why it is important that molecules that contain nitrogen are added to the soil.

questions.question: Rice, maize and wheat plants are the main carbohydrate source for more than 60% of the human population.
questions.text: Rice, maize and wheat plants are the main carbohydrate source for more than 60% of the human population.

parts.text: Describe how plants like these produce carbohydrates using materials from the environment.

Many scientists predict that, as the world's human population increases, there will be a shortage of food. Suggest and explain why an increase in human population is predicted to lead to a global human food shortage.

questions.question: Describe how air is breathed in, using the rib cage and diaphragm, so that it reaches the alveoli.
questions.text: Describe how air is breathed in, using the rib cage and diaphragm, so that it reaches the alveoli.

parts.text: Describe how air is breathed in, using the rib cage and diaphragm, so that it reaches the alveoli.

Bronchitis is a condition that can be triggered by breathing in substances that cause the bronchi to become inflamed and produce a large amount of mucus. Suggest how this condition can affect a person and explain how a person may be able to avoid developing the condition.
